summary: The review-nfrs step created a Prisma schema change (added lastSyncedAt to RepoConnection), generated a migration file, and regenerated the Prisma client — but did not run `prisma migrate deploy` to apply the migration to the database. The generated client expected the last_synced_at column; the database lacked it; every repoConnection.findUnique() call failed at runtime with 'Invalid invocation'. The step's own verification (unit tests, lint, typecheck) all passed because unit tests mock getPrisma() (never hit the real DB), typecheck uses the regenerated client types, and lint is static — no pipeline gate exercises the real database schema. The playbook has no migrate-db step. Earlier migrations from Stories 2.1 and 2.2 (add_artifact_model, add_artifact_last_modified_index) WERE applied to the database, so the pipeline normally applies migrations somewhere (likely the implement-story step), but the review-nfrs scope excursion did not follow the same pattern. This is a consequence of the already-observed nfr-review-exceeds-audit-scope, but a distinct operational failure: the schema change was half-applied (client regenerated, migration file created, DB not migrated), and no verification gate caught the drift.

Database inspection (2026-07-03T16:23Z): _prisma_migrations table lists 5 applied migrations (last: 20260703091142_add_artifact_last_modified_index); migration 20260703110000_add_repo_connection_last_synced_at absent. repo_connections table columns: id, user_id, repo_url, credential_health, created_at, updated_at — no last_synced_at. After manual `prisma migrate deploy`, column present and findUnique() succeeds. Runtime error surfaced by user launching dev server: 'Invalid prisma.repoConnection.findUnique() invocation'.
